effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanical but wide: two pricing tables unified, two flat-rate functions deleted, cost_source threaded driver->Result->Dispatch->status.json.

design_decisions.description: claude.go costSource() classification for the CLI-reported TotalCostUSD field.
options_considered: Infer subscription from an explicit reported zero (TotalCostUSD != nil && == 0) — matches documented `claude -p` behaviour when authenticated against a Claude subscription rather than an API key.

Ship every case as "unknown" until the inference is verified against a live subscription-authenticated CLI run — no positively identified, testable marker in current CLI output distinguishes 'explicit zero because subscription' from 'explicit zero because a genuinely free/no-cost turn or a protocol quirk'.
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach ratified fail-closed: ship "unknown" for both the nil and the explicit-zero case. An explicit reported zero is NOT, by itself, a positively identified subscription marker — it is also consistent with a genuinely free turn or an envelope quirk. No field in claudeEnvelope distinguishes the two today. Do NOT implement the TotalCostUSD==0 -> "subscription" inference; only a non-zero TotalCostUSD (CostSource="cli") is positively identified. Record the absent marker as a Rule 2 note in proof.json.

design_decisions.description: codex.go costSource() classification for the JSONL Usage field (codex CLI never carries a cost field in any documented shape).
options_considered: Infer subscription from Usage != nil (a turn actually completed) on the theory that codex CLI is subscription-authenticated by default and its JSONL stream never reports cost by design.

Ship every case as "unknown" until verified against a live codex binary — codex.go's own header already flags its envelope shape as unverified (SIT-deferred); Usage != nil proves a turn happened, not that it was subscription-covered rather than API-billed.
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach ratified fail-closed: ship "unknown" universally for codex.go in this slice. Usage != nil is not a positively identified subscription marker, only evidence a turn completed — codexEnvelope carries no field distinguishing subscription-covered from API-billed. Do NOT implement the Usage!=nil -> "subscription" inference. Record the absent marker as a Rule 2 note in proof.json.

design_decisions.description: Named driver.CostSource* constants (mirroring the existing ErrKind* pattern) replace scattered CostSource string literals across producers and tests.
options_considered: Named constants in internal/driver/driver.go, one per vocabulary value (provider, pricing-table, cli, subscription, unknown).
Continue with ad hoc string literals per call site.
design_decisions.human_decision: Confirmed as designed (memory-cited, pre-acknowledged in captain-proceed.md pin 5) — proceed with named constants.
